title: BackgroundService.start() not guarded by _running — duplicate starts on reconnect
description: BackgroundService._running is checked in stop() but not in start(). RelayService calls _bgService.start() (fire-and-forget) on every auth success. Auto-reconnect logic calls _doConnect() in a timer loop; each successful reconnect triggers start(), which calls _requestNotificationPermission() + invokeMethod('startService') even though the service is already running. The _running flag exists to prevent this — its asymmetric use (stop-only) will confuse the next person who reads it and assumes it gates both directions. Fix: add if (_running) return; at the top of start().
